page: Microsoft Azure E104id_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance belonging to the EIDv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 104 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 52 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, featuring a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. System memory totals 672.0 GB, providing 6.46 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 3891 GB, while no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in the PassMark CPU encryption test and strong results in multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, PassMark database operations, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5457.49 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 133.1 GB/sec. However, single-core execution is a relative bottleneck, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Large-scale LLM text generation is also a notable limitation, registering a weak performance of 2.62 tokens/sec for a 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E104id_v5 offers high resource density, combining substantial memory capacity with a large local storage allocation on a single isolated host. This profile reduces the need for external storage attachments for data-intensive operations, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training. The hardware configuration and benchmark strengths make this instance suitable for large relational databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, software compilation pipelines, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E104id_v5 is an isolated virtual machine from the EIDv5 family, powered by a 104-vCPU Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 672.0 GB of RAM and 3891 GB of bundled local storage, with no GPU resources. Benchmarks demonstrate strong multi-core capabilities, highlighted by top-tier CPU encryption performance and high memory bandwidth, alongside average single-core speeds. While large-scale LLM text generation is a performance bottleneck, the instance's high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage provide qualitative cost efficiency for data-dense workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E104id_v5 is an isolated 104-vCPU server featuring an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 672 GB of RAM, and 3891 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E104id_v5 is an isolated 104-vCPU server with 672 GB RAM and 3891 GB local storage, optimized for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-capacity server combining massive memory and local storage for demanding database and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 104 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz.
672 GB of system memory paired with 3891 GB of bundled local storage.
Strong multi-core performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth.
Average single-core performance and weak large-model LLM text generation.
Best suited for large relational databases, enterprise applications, and software compilation.
